perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Payment for the Products shall be in U.S. Dollars, or such other currency as STAAR may require in its sole discretion, and made within thirty (30) days of STAAR's invoice. Late payments shall be subject to an interest charge of 1% per month or the maximum amount permitted by applicable law, whichever is less.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 Payment for the Products shall be in U.S. Dollars, or such other currency as STAAR may require in its sole discretion, and made within ninety (90) days of STAAR's invoice. Late payments shall be subject to an interest charge of 2% per month or the maximum amount permitted by applicable law, whichever is less.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the late payment interest rate to 2% per month may violate usury laws in some states, as some states have regulations on the maximum interest rates that can be charged.
perturbation.law_explanation: In California, the maximum interest rate for loans primarily for personal, family, or household purposes is 10% per annum. A 2% monthly interest rate equates to 24% per annum, which exceeds this limit if the product is categorized as personal, family, or household.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Codes Display Text skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Group Code Section Group Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ Add To My Favorites Code Text * California Constitution - CONS ARTICLE XV USURY [SECTION 1- SECTION 1.] ( Article 15 added June 8, 1976, by Prop. 14, clause 35. Res.Ch. 5, 1976. ) SECTION 1. The rate of interest upon the loan or forbearance of any money, goods, or things in action, or on accounts after demand, shall be 7 percent per annum but it shall be competent for the parties to any loan or forbearance of any money, goods or things in action to contract in writing for a rate of interest: (1) For any loan or forbearance of any money, goods, or things in action, if the money, goods, or things in action are for use primarily for personal, family, or household purposes, at a rate not exceeding 10 percent per annum; provided, however, that any loan or forbearance of any money, goods or things in action the proceeds of which are used primarily for the purchase, construction or improvement of real property shall not be deemed to be a use primarily for personal, family or household purposes; or (2) For any loan or forbearance of any money, goods, or things in action for any use other than specified in paragraph (1), at a rate not exceeding the higher of (a) 10 percent per annum or (b) 5 percent per annum plus the rate prevailing on the 25th day of the month preceding the earlier of (i) the date of execution of the contract to make the loan or forbearance, or (ii) the date of making the loan or forbearance established by the Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco on advances to member banks under Sections 13 and 13a of the Federal Reserve Act as now in effect or hereafter from time to time amended (or if there is no such single determinable rate of advances, the closest counterpart of such rate as shall be designated by the Superintendent of Banks of the State of California unless some other person or agency is delegated such authority by the Legislature). No person, association, copartnership or corporation shall by charging any fee, bonus, commission, discount or other compensation receive from a borrower more than the interest authorized by this section upo
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies a late payment interest rate of 2% per month, which equates to 24% per annum. The law explanation suggests this exceeds the California usury limit of 10% per annum for loans primarily for personal, family, or household purposes. Scraped Law Snippet 1 confirms this limit, stating that the rate of interest shall be 7 percent per annum but it shall be competent for the parties to any loan or forbearance of any money, goods or things in action to contract in writing for a rate of interest at a rate not exceeding 10 percent per annum if for use primarily for personal, family, or household purposes. As the changed text allows for interest above the legally allowed rate, it creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10.3 Distributor shall fully document and inform STAAR in writing of any Complaints or other matters addressed in this Section 10. Documentation with respect to Complaints shall include the Product model and serial or lot number, customer contact information, and as much detail as possible regarding the nature of the Complaint. Distributor will forward to STAAR any Complaint involving significant bodily injury, illness or death resulting from use of Products within one (1) calendar day of receipt and Distributor must notify STAAR of any such incident prior to issuing any notice required by law in respect of such incident (including those to be issued to governmental or regulatory authorities). Distributor shall forward to STAAR all other Complaints within five (5) calendar days of receipt.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.3 Distributor shall fully document and inform STAAR in writing of any Complaints or other matters addressed in this Section 10. Documentation with respect to Complaints shall include the Product model and serial or lot number, customer contact information, and as much detail as possible regarding the nature of the Complaint. Distributor will forward to STAAR any Complaint involving significant bodily injury, illness or death resulting from use of Products within five (5) calendar days of receipt and Distributor must notify STAAR of any such incident prior to issuing any notice required by law in respect of such incident (including those to be issued to governmental or regulatory authorities). Distributor shall forward to STAAR all other Complaints within ten (10) calendar days of receipt.
perturbation.explanation: Delaying the reporting timeframe for complaints involving significant bodily injury or death to five calendar days may violate FDA reporting requirements for medical device adverse events. The FDA requires that manufacturers report such events much sooner.
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 803.30 outlines the reporting timelines for manufacturers regarding adverse events associated with medical devices. The modified text extends the reporting timeframe, potentially causing a violation of the FDA's requirements for timely reporting of serious adverse events.

perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the reporting timeline for serious complaints from one day to five days, and other complaints from five to ten days. The Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation suggest this delay contradicts FDA regulations regarding timely reporting of adverse events related to medical devices. While specific reporting timelines are not detailed in the provided snippets, the FDA's authority over medical device reporting is evident in Scraped Law Snippet 2, indicating a legal basis for prompt reporting requirements. This alteration directly undermines the promptness required for reporting serious incidents, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 13.2 Distributor shall, at its own expense, maintain at a minimum general and product liability coverage in the Territory of at least US$2 million per occurrence, US$5 million in the aggregate. On a separate endorsement, Distributor shall name STAAR as an additional named insured. Such separate endorsement shall indicate that Distributor's insurance is primary and that STAAR's coverage as an additional named insured is not contributory. Each such insurance policy and endorsement shall provide that the insurance will not be canceled or reduces without at least thirty (30) days' prior written notice to STAAR. On request, Distributor shall provide STAAR with copies or certificates of all such insurance policies.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.2 Distributor shall, at its own expense, maintain at a minimum general and product liability coverage in the Territory of at least US$2 million per occurrence, US$5 million in the aggregate. On a separate endorsement, Distributor shall name STAAR as an additional named insured. Such separate endorsement shall indicate that Distributor's insurance is primary and that STAAR's coverage as an additional named insured is not contributory. Each such insurance policy and endorsement shall provide that the insurance will not be canceled or reduces without at least ten (10) days' prior written notice to STAAR. On request, Distributor shall provide STAAR with copies or certificates of all such insurance policies.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the insurance cancellation notice period to 10 days contradicts standard insurance regulations that often require a minimum of 30 days' notice for cancellation or reduction of coverage.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Insurance Code § 677.2 generally requires a 30-day notice period for cancellation of a policy. Reducing this to 10 days is a violation of California law.

GENERAL RULES GOVERNING INSURANCE [100 - 1879.8] ( Division 1 enacted by Stats. 1935, Ch. 145. ) PART 1. THE CONTRACT [100 - 679.75] ( Part 1 enacted by Stats. 1935, Ch. 145. ) CHAPTER 11. Cancellation and Failure to Renew Certain Property Insurance [675 - 679.7] ( Chapter 11 added by Stats. 1970, Ch. 313. ) 677.2. (a) This section applies only to policies covered by Section 675.5. (b) A notice of cancellation shall be in writing and shall be delivered or mailed to the producer of record, provided that the producer of record is not an employee of the insurer, and to the named insured at the mailing address shown on the policy. The time periods and procedures in subdivision (a) of Section 1013 of the Code of Civil Procedure shall be applicable if the notice is mailed. The notice of cancellation shall include the effective date of the cancellation and the reasons for the cancellation. (c) The notice of cancellation shall be given at least 30 days prior to the effective date of the cancellation, except that in the case of cancellation for nonpayment of premiums or for fraud the notice shall be given no less than 10 days prior to the effective date of the cancellation. Notice of a proposed cancellation pursuant to subdivision (d) of Sect
perturbation.justification: The modified text reduces the insurance cancellation notice period from 30 days to 10 days. The Law Explanation states that California Insurance Code § 677.2 generally requires a 30-day notice period for cancellation of a policy, and reducing this to 10 days is a violation of California law. Scraped Law Snippet 1 supports this, stating in 677.2 (c): "The notice of cancellation shall be given at least 30 days prior to the effective date of the cancellation..." The changed text therefore contradicts the legal requirement of a 30-day notice, creating a clear violation.
